content_parts.text:
You are an expert at extracting calendar events from emails.

**Current Date:** 2026-02-21

**Email Metadata:**
- Subject: Welcome New YSC Board Members
- From: YSC <info@ysc.org>
- Date Sent: 2026-02-17T03:54:48Z

**Instructions:**
1. Analyze the email content and any attachments (including PDFs, images, calendars)
2. Extract ALL calendar events mentioned — meetings, appointments, parties, closures, themed days, deadlines, etc.
3. For each event, extract:
   - Title: clear, descriptive event name
   - Start date/time (parse relative dates like "tomorrow", "next Friday")
   - End date/time (if mentioned)
   - Location: physical address, room, venue, or virtual meeting link. If no specific location is mentioned in the email, set to null. Do NOT infer or guess locations from the organization name or event type.
   - Full description with all relevant details
   - Whether it's an all-day event (true/false)
   - Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on clarity of information
   - Importance: "action_required" or "fyi" (see classification below)
   - Recurrence rule: for recurring events (weekly meetings, monthly reviews, etc.), provide an RFC 5545 RRULE string (e.g., "RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO"). Null for one-time events.

**Importance Classification:**
- **action_required**: Events that require the recipient to take action or change their schedule.
  Examples: closures (school closed, office closed), early dismissals, half days, deadlines,
  appointments, meetings, conferences, registration deadlines, performances to attend.
- **fyi**: Informational events that are nice to know but don't require action.
  Examples: themed dress-up days (pajama day, hat day), staff birthdays, spirit weeks,
  movie days, classroom activities the child participates in without parent involvement.

**Important:**
- If NO calendar events are found, set events_found=false and return empty events list
- Parse dates carefully using the current date as context
- Include uncertainty in confidence scores (e.g., 0.7 if time is ambiguous)
- Extract ALL events including themed days and informational items — classify them as "fyi"
- For PDFs: extract events from calendar grids, flyers, and schedules

**Examples of events:**
✓ Birthday party invitations (action_required)
✓ Doctor/dentist appointments (action_required)
✓ Meeting requests (action_required)
✓ School closures (action_required)
✓ Themed dress-up days at school (fyi)
✓ Staff birthdays (fyi)
✓ Conference registrations (action_required)

**NOT events (do NOT extract these):**
✗ Commercial transactions: receipts, shipping notifications, payment reminders, subscription renewals, promotional offers
✗ Administrative notices: terms of service updates, privacy policies, password resets, account statements, survey requests
✗ Content without actionable dates: newsletters, social media notifications, general announcements without specific event details
✗ Emails where event details (date, time) must be inferred or guessed from context — only extract events with explicitly stated dates and times
✗ Estimated delivery/shipping dates — these are logistical estimates, not scheduled appointments
✗ Completed/past events in order confirmations or pickup receipts
✗ Financial statement dates, billing cycles, and payment due dates
✗ Government comment periods and regulatory deadlines (unless a specific hearing or meeting)

**Extraction rules:**
- Multi-day events (e.g., 3-day conference) should be ONE event with start/end spanning the full duration, not separate per-day events
- Do NOT create separate events for RSVP deadlines, early bird deadlines, or registration cutoffs — note them in the main event's description instead
- Only extract events with explicitly stated dates — do NOT infer dates from holidays, context, or subject lines

**Email Body:**
Four new members joined the YSC board

Newsletter, February 16, 2026

Welcome New Board Members

On February 1st we had our annual meeting at the Swedish American Hall. You can read more about the meeting below, but I would really like to thank Jeanette Flodell for having been our president for the past three years. Even though being president is somewhat of a marathon, Jeanette did an amazing sprint towards the finish line by being in charge of, and putting together, an amazing 75 year anniversary party back in October. Thank you Jeanette!!!

I would also like to welcome four new people to the board, Anne Buike, Christopher Tevren, Elsa Putur and Polina Pribytkova. Thank you all so much for stepping up and wanting to help run the club. We've already put a number of new exciting events on the calendar, so save the dates, for those that are still being worked out.

Finally I'd like to remind everyone that the whole Clear Lake Cabin is available for rent for only $100 per night during the winter. I spent this long weekend up there with a number of friends and it's a great and different experience to visit the cabin in the winter. We now have three bedrooms with beds for 8 people, and with a fire in the fireplace you get some truly Scandinavian hygge. There's also plenty of things to do and explore around the lake, so check it out, you can't find a better deal!

Peter Nordström (your new/old president)
Ps. On Friday Jan 30 we had a fun Full moon hike. Great to see so many people joining on short notice.

Annual Meeting at Swedish American Hall - Feb 1, 2026

Before we dove into the official business, the afternoon kicked off at 1:00 PM with a social hour. About 40 people showed up and it was fantastic to see so many familiar faces and welcome several newcomers over drinks and mingling. Whether it was catching up on Tahoe ski conditions or sharing stories from the holidays, that first moment of …

Upcoming Events

Happy Hour Above the City in the Transamerica Building

February 19

Join Polina and Mary for a truly special YSC Happy Hour experience, one you won’t want to miss. This February, we’re heading inside the iconic Transamerica Building for exclusive access to a 48th-floor bar with breathtaking views of San Francisco.

When: Thursday, February 19, 5:30 PM
What: Evening Happy Hour (timed entry required)
Where: Transamerica Building – 48th Floor

This event is by registration only and must be coordinated directly through Mary in advance! Due to building security, all guests must be checked in at a designated arrival time to gain access upstairs.
Available arrival time slots:
• 5:30 PM
• 6:00 PM
• 6:30 PM
If you’re interested in attending, you must RSVP on partiful under the following link, where you can also read more details: https://partiful.com/e/g1hU5HXmUnfJwxpW8u8M?c=fLU6roGc

March 19th: YSC Happy Hour at The Royal Cuckoo

YSC Happy Hour at The Royal Cuckoo – Join us on Thursday, March 19th, from 5:30 PM at one of SF’s most unique cocktail bars. Enjoy exclusive drink specials, a cozy vintage atmosphere, and some of the best Aquavit in town, courtesy of owner Paul’s Danish roots. Great drinks, great vibes, and great company and live music—don’t miss it!

March 28th: YSC Bus Trip to Wine Country (save the date)

Bus trip to wine country, details coming soon.

April 4th: Joint YSC-SSCC Easter Luncheon (Save the date)

Come join the YSC and SSCC for a traditional Scandinavian Luncheon. More info to follow. If you would like to help out organizing this event, please fill out this form.

April 5th: Joint YSC-SSCC Kids Easter & Crafts (save the date)

Kids and family friendly event at the Scandinavian School and Cultural Center

Tahoe cabin reservations

The Tahoe winter reservations have now been open for a while, and we are seeing a lot of bookings come in. To make the system as fair as possible for everyone, we want to kindly remind everyone about the booking rules:

- Single membership allows you to make reservations for ONE room, ONE stay at a time. Once your stay is over, you can then make your next reservation.
- Family membership allows reservations for TWO rooms, ONE stay at a time, i.e. on the same dates.
- We do not allow multiple reservations for different dates. Once your current reservation is over, you can go online and book your next one.
- IMPORTANT: You do need to make sure that you include the correct # of people that will stay in each room in the reservation. If you need to add extra people to the reservation, email info@ysc.org, and let us know what the adjustment should be. Note that there is a max # of individuals allowed per room, as outlined in the room descriptions on the reservation page.

Please email tahoe@ysc.org if you have any questions about the cabin or the reservation system.

Young Scandinavians Club
PO Box 640610
San Francisco, CA 94164

CalendarEvent.description: A single calendar event extracted from an email.

start_datetime.description: The event start date and time (ISO 8601)
start_datetime.title: Start Datetime

end_datetime.description: The event end date and time (ISO 8601)
end_datetime.title: End Datetime

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event
all_day.title: All Day

description.description: Detailed description of the event with all relevant information
description.title: Description

confidence.description: Confidence score from 0.0 to 1.0 indicating extraction certainty
confidence.title: Confidence

importance.description: Event importance: 'action_required' for closures, schedule changes, deadlines the user must act on; 'fyi' for themed days, birthdays, informational items that are nice to know
importance.title: Importance

recurrence_rule.description: RFC 5545 RRULE for recurring events (e.g., 'RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO'). Null for one-time events.
recurrence_rule.title: Recurrence Rule

CalendarEvent.title: CalendarEvent

events_found.description: Whether any calendar events were found in the email
events_found.title: Events Found
